commodities.name: ginger
terms: fresh fruits and vegetables (including fresh ginger)

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to the rhizome of commercial varieties of ginger grown from Zingiber officinale Roscoe, of the Zingiberaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Ginger for industrial processing is excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to the rhizome of commercial varieties of ginger grown from Zingiber officinale Roscoe, of the Zingiberaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Ginger for industrial processing is excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to the rhizome of commercial varieties of ginger grown from Zingiber officinale Roscoe, of the Zingiberaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Ginger for industrial processing is excluded.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Ginger in this class must be of superior quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The roots must be cleaned, well shaped and free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Ginger in this class must be of superior quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The roots must be cleaned, well shaped and free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Ginger in this class must be of good quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The roots must be firm, without evidence of shrivelling or dehydration and without evidence of sprouting. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5
level=1

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
UNIFORMITY
sections.text:
The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only ginger of the same origin, variety, and/or commercial type , quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.
The weight of the heaviest hand (rhizome) may not be more than twice the weight of the lightest hand (rhizome) in the same package.

page_spans.text:
The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only ginger of the same origin, variety, and/or commercial type , quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.
The weight of the heaviest hand (rhizome) may not be more than twice the weight of the lightest hand (rhizome) in the same package.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
Ginger must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of pape r or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Ginger shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.text: Each package must be ar the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the ginger. Pack ages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR GINGER (CODEX STAN 218-1999)
pages.summary: Página contém o título do documento e cabeçalho. Estrutura hierárquica identificada com duas seções principais: '1. DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' e '2. PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY', com várias subseções numeradas até nível 3. Algumas seções continuam na próxima página (continues_on_next_page=true), indicando conteúdo incompleto para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 218
pages.summary: Página contém as seções '3. PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING' e '4. PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES' com suas respectivas subseções numeradas, texto associado corretamente e sem continuidade para outras páginas.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 218
pages.summary: Página mapeada com as seções principais 5 e 6, suas subseções e texto associado corretamente. Identificada continuidade da seção 5 para página seguinte. Estrutura numerada claramente respeitada.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 218
pages.summary: Página contém as seções 6.2.4 e 6.2.5 dentro da continuidade da seção 6 iniciada anteriormente, além das seções 7 (CONTAMINANTS) e 8 (HYGIENE) com suas subseções e texto associado. Seção 6 é continuação da página anterior; as outras seções começam nesta página. Todos os títulos estão visíveis, e o texto está associado corretamente para suporte a RAG.
